title: Statin use to prevent the development of cardiovascular diseases
subtitle: Share of people without a history of cardiovascular diseases, but are eligible for statin therapy, who report using [statins](#dod:statins) to prevent the development of [cardiovascular diseases](#dod:cvd). This is shown for lower- and middle-income countries.
note: Eligibility included people without prior cardiovascular disease who either (1) have diagnosed diabetes or (2) have a 10-year cardiovascular risk over 20% based on the 2019 WHO guidelines.
caption:
Statin use to prevent the development of cardiovascular diseases

Share of people without a history of cardiovascular diseases, but are eligible for statin therapy, who report using statins to prevent the development of cardiovascular diseases. This is shown for lower- and middle-income countries.

This horizontal bar chart displays 2019 data on statin usage among eligible populations in lower- and middle-income countries. The chart ranks nations by the share of people without a history of cardiovascular diseases who report using statins for prevention. Eligibility for this metric included individuals without prior cardiovascular disease who either had diagnosed diabetes or a 10-year cardiovascular risk exceeding 20% based on 2019 WHO guidelines. Jordan holds the highest position with a usage rate of 29%, followed closely by Lebanon at 25.4% and Iran at 24.7%. A distinct gap separates these top three from the next tier, where Algeria reports 14.1%, Mexico 11.3%, and Uganda 10.1%. The middle section of the chart shows a cluster of countries with usage rates between roughly 6% and 7.3%, including Mongolia, Belarus, Bangladesh, Vietnam, Morocco, and Sudan. Below this group, Georgia, Botswana, and Myanmar fall within the 3% to 4% range. The lowest values appear at the bottom of the list, with Armenia at 1.7%, Nepal at 1.5%, Afghanistan at 0.9%, and Zambia at 0.6%. Kenya, Ethiopia, and Burkina Faso are recorded at 0%, indicating no reported usage among the eligible population in those specific datasets. The visual arrangement highlights a wide disparity in preventive statin adoption, ranging from nearly one-third of the eligible population in Jordan to zero in several African nations.
